operating_state: Tesla achieved record 2021 revenue of $53.8 billion, up 71%, driven by 936,222 vehicle deliveries with Model 3 and Model Y production scaling across Gigafactory Shanghai and new facilities under construction in Berlin and Texas. Energy generation and storage revenue grew 40% from expanded Megapack and solar deployments. The company relocated corporate headquarters to Gigafactory Texas while expanding beyond automotive into energy storage, solar products, and autonomous driving capabilities through direct-to-consumer networks globally.
cost_structure: Automotive gross margin expanded 370 basis points to 29.3% primarily from favorable production mix as Gigafactory Shanghai ramped with lower localized costs, offsetting rising raw material and logistics expenses from global supply chain pressures. Net income reached $5.5 billion representing a $4.8 billion year-over-year improvement driven by production efficiencies and operational scaling. Energy storage margins deteriorated to negative 4.6% during Solar Roof manufacturing ramp.
material_events: Tesla invested $1.5 billion in bitcoin during first quarter 2021, subsequently selling portions while recording gains and impairment losses, establishing $1.99 billion in digital asset holdings. Equipment testing began at Gigafactory Berlin and Texas facilities in late 2021 with Model Y production commencing at the Texas location. The company settled SEC enforcement actions paying civil penalties and implementing enhanced governance measures while construction progressed on the Megafactory in Lathrop, California.
macro_exposures: Global semiconductor shortages disrupted production requiring alternative component sourcing and manufacturing workarounds. Foreign exchange exposures increased with cash held in foreign currencies generating favorable fluctuations during 2021. COVID-19 pandemic effects persisted through supply chain constraints, port congestion, labor shortages, and increased logistics costs while rising raw material prices for lithium, nickel, and cobalt pressured battery procurement costs.
forward_outlook: Management projects annual capital expenditures supporting manufacturing expansion across three continents and proprietary battery cell development. Production growth depends on successful facility ramps at Gigafactory Berlin and Texas plus high-volume battery manufacturing with lower costs to reduce supplier dependence. The company expects continued positive operating cash flow generation exceeding capital expenditure levels to self-fund operations and expanded product roadmap including Tesla Semi and Cybertruck.
uncertainty_profile: Production ramp uncertainties exist for new international facilities amid concurrent global projects and potential component constraints requiring sustained manufacturing workarounds. Digital asset holdings subject to impairment accounting treatment with no upward revaluation until sale creating potential earnings volatility from market fluctuations. Outstanding debt obligations include binding capital commitments with required spending at facilities, while regulatory challenges persist regarding direct-to-consumer vehicle sales and autonomous driving feature scrutiny.
